data.title: Volex Targets Main Market Listing and £40m Share Buyback
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Volex plc ( (GB:VLX) ) has provided an announcement.

Volex plc confirmed it intends to move its listing from London’s AIM to the Main Market, targeting admission before 4 August 2026, which would allow potential inclusion in the FTSE 250 index. The shift, which does not require shareholder approval, is subject to FCA and London Stock Exchange clearance, after which trading on AIM will cease and investors are advised to consider the implications.

Alongside the planned move, the board has launched a share buyback programme of up to £40 million to return capital to shareholders. Volex will also hold a capital markets event on 22 April 2026 to present new medium-term growth targets to institutional investors and analysts, signalling a bid to strengthen its profile and appeal in public markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:VLX) stock is a Buy with a £536.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Volex plc stock, see the GB:VLX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on VLX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VLX is a Outperform.

Volex plc’s strong financial performance and positive earnings call sentiment are the primary drivers of its overall score. The company’s strategic focus on growth sectors like electric vehicles and data centers, along with stable financials, positions it well for future success. Technical analysis and valuation are neutral, indicating a balanced risk-reward profile.

To see Spark’s full report on VLX stock, click here.

More about Volex plc

Volex plc is a UK-headquartered integrated manufacturer specialising in mission-critical power and data connectivity solutions. Serving international blue-chip customers, it operates 23 manufacturing facilities and supplies OEMs and EMS companies in sectors including electric vehicles, consumer electricals, medical, complex industrial technology and off-highway equipment.

The company focuses on delivering power and connectivity products that support applications from household devices to life-saving medical equipment. Its global operations span 25 countries, leveraging localised sales teams and distributor partners to reach key end-markets worldwide.

Average Trading Volume: 565,383

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £879.7M

data.title: Kazera Global Reshapes Board to Drive Mining Projects and Production Push
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Kazera Global plc ( (GB:KZG) ) just unveiled an update.

Kazera Global has overhauled its board, appointing major shareholder Richard Jennings as executive director and interim chief executive for six months, promoting Geoff Eyre to non-executive chairman, and bringing in mining geologist Dr Johan Hattingh (subject to due diligence) and adviser Paul Dulieu to strengthen technical and governance capabilities. The reshaped leadership is tasked with accelerating progress on South African heavy mineral sands projects, resolving issues at the Aftan asset in Namibia, securing the pivotal 2A mining right, and finalising a new operating partner to optimise production and improve product quality and volumes.

The company portrays the reset as aligning its two largest shareholders more closely with strategy execution while adding specialist technical and operational expertise to support an expected period of heightened operational and corporate activity. Management signals a focus on unlocking value from its South African and Namibian assets through faster movement to cash-generative production and potential monetisation of non-core projects, positioning Kazera for improved long-term returns if execution matches the upgraded leadership structure.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:KZG) stock is a Hold with a £0.67 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Kazera Global plc stock, see the GB:KZG Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on KZG Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, KZG is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by weak financial performance (zero revenue in 2025, large losses, and ongoing cash burn) and bearish technicals (below key moving averages with negative MACD). Valuation provides limited support because the negative P/E is driven by losses and no dividend yield is available.

To see Spark’s full report on KZG stock, click here.

More about Kazera Global plc

Kazera Global plc is an AIM-quoted investment company focused on mining and natural resources, with key assets in heavy mineral sands, diamonds and the Aftan project in Namibia. The company’s portfolio is centred on projects in South Africa and Namibia, where it aims to advance mining operations toward sustainable, cash-generative production and enhance product quality and volumes.

data.title: BW Energy Wins 25-Year Extension of Key Gabon Offshore Licence
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

BW Energy Ltd. ( (DE:6BW) ) has provided an announcement.

BW Energy has secured a 25-year extension to the Dussafu Marin production licence offshore Gabon, pushing expiry from 2028 to 2053 and solidifying the company’s long-term operational footing in one of its core producing assets. The extension underscores a strong partnership with Gabonese authorities and gives the company greater certainty for production, investment planning and reserve development in the area.

The new licence horizon supports ongoing projects such as MaBoMo Phase 2 and the planned Bourdon development, while enhancing the prospects for infrastructure-led growth across adjacent Niosi and Guduma licences operated by BW Energy. By locking in this extended tenure, BW Energy is better positioned to expand output, optimize use of existing offshore infrastructure and contribute to sustained economic activity in Gabon, which could benefit both the company’s growth trajectory and its host country stakeholders.

More about BW Energy Ltd.

BW Energy is a fast-growing independent oil and gas company focused on low-risk, phased developments of proven offshore reservoirs, leveraging existing infrastructure and capital-efficient execution. The company owns and operates production, development and exploration assets in Gabon, Brazil and Namibia, with substantial 2P reserves and 2C resources underpinning planned growth in oil output over the coming years.

Average Trading Volume: 134,289

Current Market Cap: NOK15.06B

Key Petroleum Limited ( (AU:KEY) ) has provided an announcement.

Key Petroleum Limited has completed a capital raising of AU$176,659.76 through a placement of 3,045,858 fully paid ordinary shares to sophisticated investors at an issue price of AU$0.058 per share. The shares will be issued under the company’s existing placement capacity and are expected to be allotted by 28 April 2026, subject to cleared funds.

Proceeds from the placement will be used to acquire assets with development potential, fund maintenance and development of existing assets, and provide additional working capital. The transaction is expected to strengthen Key Petroleum’s balance sheet and support its growth strategy by enabling further investment in its asset base and operational activities.

More about Key Petroleum Limited

Key Petroleum Limited is an Australian-listed energy company focused on the exploration, development and maintenance of petroleum assets. The company targets assets with development upside, seeking to enhance production potential and optimize the value of its existing portfolio within the domestic energy market.

Resources & Energy Group Limited has issued 10,714,284 new ordinary shares at 1.4 cents each to its directors, following shareholder approval at a meeting held on 7 April 2026. data.title: Anteris adjusts CDI and common stock balances through cross-market transfers
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Anteris Technologies ( (AU:AVR) ).

Anteris Technologies Global Corp. reported its March 2026 CDI statement, showing 15,608,676 CDIs on issue over quoted securities at month-end, down from 15,754,513 in February. The overall change reflects a net transfer of 145,837 securities between CDIs on the ASX and common stock held and quoted on Nasdaq.

Correspondingly, total common stock without CDIs increased to 81,623,378 shares, up from 81,477,541 in the prior month, matching the net CDI outflow. The company’s restricted stock units, options, and warrants remained unchanged, indicating that recent capital structure movements are primarily due to cross-market conversions rather than new equity issuance or dilution.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:AVR) stock is a Buy with a A$13.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Anteris Technologies stock, see the AU:AVR Stock Forecast page.

More about Anteris Technologies

Anteris Technologies Global Corp., listed on the ASX under code AVR, issues CHESS Depositary Interests (CDIs) over its common stock, which is also quoted on Nasdaq. The company’s capital structure includes common stock, restricted stock units, options expiring on various dates at various prices, and warrants, reflecting a mix of listed and unlisted equity instruments.

data.title: Westpac trims substantial stake in Pengana Private Equity Trust
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Westpac Banking ( (AU:WBC) ) has provided an announcement.

Westpac Banking Corporation and its associated entities have reduced their substantial holding in Pengana Private Equity Trust, lowering their voting power from 6.80% to 5.71%. The change reflects adjustments made primarily through BT Funds Management and Westpac Financial Services, which manage superannuation funds and investment schemes that hold PE1 units.

Following a series of transactions between January 2025 and April 2026, Westpac’s funds now control 15,248,495 ordinary units in the Pengana Private Equity Trust. While Westpac remains a significant investor, the reduced stake marginally lessens its influence over the trust’s unitholder decisions, signalling a recalibration of exposure to this listed private equity vehicle rather than a full strategic exit.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:WBC) stock is a Sell with a A$34.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Westpac Banking stock, see the AU:WBC Stock Forecast page.

More about Westpac Banking

Westpac Banking Corporation is a major Australian financial services group that operates as a diversified banking institution. Through its subsidiaries, including BT Funds Management and Westpac Financial Services, it provides banking, superannuation, funds management, and investment services to retail, institutional, and wealth-management clients.

data.title: Tata Technologies Confirms SEBI Compliance on Q4 2026 Dematerialization
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tata Technologies Limited ( (IN:TATATECH) ) has issued an announcement.

Tata Technologies Limited has reported that, for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, all details of securities dematerialized or rematerialized have been duly furnished to the stock exchanges where its shares are listed, in compliance with Regulation 74(5) of the SEBI (Depositories and Participants) Regulations, 2018. The company’s registrar and transfer agent, MUFG Intime India Private Limited, has independently confirmed that these dematerialization details were also provided to both the depositories and the relevant exchanges, underscoring continued adherence to regulatory and disclosure requirements.

This procedural disclosure reinforces Tata Technologies’ focus on maintaining transparent capital market practices and robust compliance standards. While operationally routine, such confirmations help reassure investors and regulators about the integrity of the company’s shareholding records and the smooth functioning of its dematerialization processes.

More about Tata Technologies Limited

Tata Technologies Limited is an engineering and product development digital services company headquartered in Pune, India. It focuses on providing engineering, research and development, and technology solutions primarily to automotive, aerospace, and industrial clients, supporting their product innovation and manufacturing transformation needs worldwide.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Hi-Tech Gears Confirms SEBI-Compliant Dematerialisation of Shares for March Quarter
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hi-Tech Gears Limited ( (IN:HITECHGEAR) ) has shared an announcement.

Hi-Tech Gears Limited has notified the stock exchanges that its registrar and share transfer agent, MAS Services Limited, has certified compliance with Regulation 74(5) of the SEBI (Depositories and Participants) Regulations, 2018 for the quarter ended March 31, 2026. The certificate confirms that all securities received for dematerialisation during the period were processed, mutilated and cancelled after verification, and reflected in the depository and member records within the prescribed timelines, underscoring the company’s adherence to regulatory norms and robust shareholder servicing.

MAS Services also confirmed that the names of the depositories have been substituted as registered owners in the members’ register and that updates were communicated to all relevant stock exchanges within 15 days. This procedural confirmation supports the integrity and efficiency of Hi-Tech Gears’ share dematerialisation process, providing reassurance to investors and regulators about the company’s compliance and record-keeping standards.

More about Hi-Tech Gears Limited

Hi-Tech Gears Limited is an India-based manufacturer in the automotive and engineering components sector, operating production facilities in Haryana and Rajasthan along with subsidiaries in Canada and the U.S. The company focuses on supplying precision gear and transmission components to domestic and international original equipment manufacturers, supported by an established network of works and a corporate office in Gurugram.

data.title: Vitura Health Director Gerard Fogarty AO Steps Down with Minimal Shareholdings
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Cronos Australia Ltd. ( (AU:VIT) ) is now available.

Vitura Health Limited has announced that director Gerard Fogarty AO has ceased to be a director of the company as of 7 April 2026, with no securities held in his own name at the time of his departure. His indirect interest in the company is limited to 342,428 ordinary shares held by his spouse, Amanda Dorothy Fogarty, and there are no disclosed interests in any related contracts, indicating a relatively clean change in the company’s board composition with limited direct implications for its capital structure.

The notice formalises Fogarty’s exit under ASX listing rule 3.19A.3 and Corporations Act disclosure requirements, underscoring Vitura Health’s adherence to governance and transparency standards in reporting director interests. The change may prompt stakeholders to watch for future board appointments or strategic shifts, but the absence of complex shareholdings or contract ties suggests minimal immediate disruption to governance or shareholder alignment.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:VIT) stock is a Hold with a A$0.04 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cronos Australia Ltd. stock, see the AU:VIT Stock Forecast page.
